NORTHROOT
ProductSystemsDocsJournalExamples

Section

ProductSystemsDocsJournalExamples
Journal

Clean Reset Note

A field note on the current workspace shape: small kernel, inspectable examples, source-owned docs.

The current Northroot workspace is intentionally small.

The repository owns the public trust kernel, the first evidence example, and the docs that explain core invariants. The site lives beside those materials as a publication layer, not as a separate product shell.

Current Shape

  • Rust crates for canonicalization and journal verification.
  • CLI commands for validation, hashing, canonicalization, event IDs, and receipt verification.
  • JSON/JSONL examples for the first public evidence bundle.
  • Static public site pages that point back to source-owned material.

Implementation Notes

The next useful work is not a bigger homepage. It is more source-backed material: examples, invariants, command walkthroughs, and field notes tied to source.

Operational Lessons

Identity should grow from evidence. The site can set the direction, but the repository has to supply the substance.

Source

northroot/README.mdnorthroot/docs/developer/architecture.md

Explanations live here. Canonical contracts remain in source.
